Description: Availability of high-resolution RGB images (1 = Yes; 0 = No)
Presence of multispectral satellite imagery (1 = Yes; 0 = No)
Usage of thermal imaging to analyze crop temperature variations (1 = Yes; 0 = No)
Availability of time-series crop images (1 = Yes; 0 = No)
Spatial resolution of the captured image (in meters)
Unique field location identifiers based on GPS mapping
Categorized field boundary types (1; 2; 3)
Altitude of the agricultural plot (in meters)
Percentage of plant canopy coverage over soil
Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (0 to 1 scale)
Soil-Adjusted Vegetation Index for correcting soil effects
Chlorophyll level measurement to assess crop health
Ratio of total leaf area per ground area unit
Stress level assessment (0 = No Stress; 100 = Extreme Stress)
Temperature (°C) recorded at the field location
Relative humidity (%) in the agricultural environment
Precipitation levels (mm) measured over a period
Wind speed (m/s) affecting plant structure
Soil water retention percentage
pH level of the soil sample collected
Organic carbon and nutrients present in soil (g/kg)
Indicator for pest-affected areas (1 = Yes; 0 = No)
Percentage of weed coverage in the field
Severity of pest damage on crops (0-100)
Growth phase of the crop (1 = Early; 2 = Vegetative; 3 = Flowering; 4 = Maturity)
Predicted yield of the crop (kg/ha)
Type of cultivated crop (e.g. Wheat; Maize; Rice)
Whether ground truth labels are available (1 = Yes; 0 = No)
Number of detected objects in a satellite image
Water availability for irrigation (L/s)
Presence of drainage features (1 = Yes; 0 = No)
Target variable indicating crop condition (0 = Unhealthy; 1 = Healthy)
212019 samples
The dataset is intentionally unbalanced across various attributes (e.g. pest-affected areas are only present in ~15% of cases)
60% Wheat; 30% Maize; 10% Rice
70% Healthy crops; 30% Unhealthy crops
Collected from different regions with varying climate conditions; ensuring generalizability
